title: Rental Property Rekeying in Alachua, FL: Best Practices for Landlords and Property Managers
excerpt: Protect your Alachua rental between tenants with smart rekeying practices. Learn when to rekey, what to expect, and how Be Secure Locksmith can help.
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: Rental Property Rekeying Alachua FL: Landlord Best Practices
metaDescription: Protect your Alachua rental between tenants with smart rekeying practices. Learn when to rekey, what to expect, and how Be Secure Locksmith can help.
body: Every tenant turnover creates a security gap. Keys may have been copied, shared with roommates, or handed to contractors over the course of a lease. Rekeying your Alachua rental property before a new tenant moves in closes that gap at a fraction of the cost of replacing locks entirely.

Whether you manage a single-family home near US-441 or a portfolio of units across the area, a consistent rekeying policy keeps your properties secure, your liability low, and your new tenants confident from day one.
Why Rekeying Should Happen at Every Turnover

Florida law does not always require landlords to rekey between tenants, but best practice strongly supports doing it every time. When a lease ends, you cannot know how many copies of the key were made or where they ended up. A thorough rekey eliminates that uncertainty before a new tenant moves in.

Rekeying replaces the internal pins of the lock cylinder so the old key no longer works. The existing hardware stays in place, which keeps costs down. For landlords managing multiple units, professional lock rekeying services reset all entry points on a unit in a single visit. Having a dated rekey record also gives landlords a clear paper trail if a dispute over property access ever arises.
What Entry Points Should Be Rekeyed?
A thorough turnover rekey goes beyond the front door. Every keyed entry point on the property should be addressed, including:

• Rear and side doors
  • Garage entry doors
  • Mailbox locks
  • Storage units or laundry rooms attached to the rental

Missing a secondary entry point is a common oversight that leaves a gap in the property’s security. Bundling all entry points into one service call keeps turnover timelines tight. We can also assess lock condition during each visit and flag worn hardware, loose cylinders, or door alignment issues that need attention before move-in.
Should Landlords Consider a Master Key System?

For property managers handling multiple units, a master key system simplifies day-to-day access without compromising tenant privacy. Each tenant holds a key that works only their unit, while the property manager carries a master that operates all units in the system.

This setup is especially useful in multi-unit buildings with frequent maintenance access. When turnover happens, only the tenant-side pins are changed, and the master key hierarchy stays intact. We build these systems on-site and test each key and cylinder combination before completing the job.
Is Rekeying Enough, or Should You Upgrade the Locks?
Rekeying is the right move in most turnover situations, but certain circumstances call for a full lock upgrade:

• Hardware is visibly worn or was damaged during the tenancy
  • Locks are older models without modern security features
  • You want to eliminate physical keys entirely going forward
  • The property is being upgraded to attract higher-quality tenants

If you are considering keyless entry, smart lock installation gives landlords a practical advantage at turnover. You can change the access code between tenants instead of cutting new keys, and temporary codes can be issued to maintenance crews without handing out physical copies. We can assess your locks on-site and give you a clear recommendation without any pressure to spend more than the situation requires.
How to Build a Reliable Rekeying Schedule

The most efficient approach is to treat rekeying as a fixed step in your turnover checklist alongside cleaning and painting. Schedule the rekey for the day after a tenant vacates so the property is secured before contractors or showing appointments begin. Waiting until move-in day creates an unnecessary window of risk.

We work as a mobile locksmith throughout Alachua and surrounding communities, which means we come directly to the property. There is no need to remove hardware or drop anything off at a shop. We carry the tools and parts to rekey most residential locks on the first visit, keeping your turnaround time short.

Maintaining a log of each rekey date, the technician who performed the work, and the number of keys issued to each new tenant supports solid recordkeeping throughout a tenancy. You can find the full range of support we offer on the property management locksmith services page.
Keep Your Alachua Rentals Secure With Every Turnover

Rekeying at each vacancy removes the uncertainty of past key holders, protects your property from unauthorized access, and signals to new tenants that you take the security of their home seriously.
